question: Quand utiliser Lombok vs Java Records ?

principles.text: Lombok pour JPA entities (@Getter @Setter @Builder, JAMAIS @Data/@EqualsAndHashCode sur entities), @RequiredArgsConstructor services, @Slf4j. Records pour DTOs simples immutables (MapStruct 1.5+ les supporte). Records ne peuvent PAS etre des JPA entities (besoin de no-arg constructor + mutabilite).

sources.name: JPA Buddy — Lombok Best Practices
sources.quote: Never use @Data on JPA entities — breaks equals/hashCode with proxies

sources.name: Thorben Janssen — Lombok+Hibernate Pitfalls
sources.quote: @EqualsAndHashCode on entities causes proxy comparison bugs

sources.name: Baeldung — Record vs Lombok
sources.quote: Records are ideal for DTOs, Lombok for mutable entities

sources.name: JetBrains State of Java 2025
sources.quote: Lombok used by 67% of Java developers, records adoption growing

sources.name: Baeldung — Records with JPA
sources.quote: Records cannot be JPA entities — no no-arg constructor, immutable

java-spring-boot.recommendation: Lombok pour entities JPA: @Getter @Setter @Builder @NoArgsConstructor @AllArgsConstructor. JAMAIS @Data sur entities. @RequiredArgsConstructor pour injection dans services. @Slf4j pour logging. Records pour tous les DTOs simples.
java-spring-boot.implementation: Configurer lombok.config a la racine: lombok.addLombokGeneratedAnnotation=true (pour exclusion Jacoco). Entities: @Getter @Setter @Builder @NoArgsConstructor @AllArgsConstructor. Services: @RequiredArgsConstructor + champs final. DTOs: Java Records avec MapStruct 1.5+.

sources.name: JetBrains State of Java 2025
sources.quote: 67% Lombok adoption, records growing for DTOs
